tool.name: Sampling Map
tool.command: mkdir -p outputs && Rscript '${__tool_directory__}/map_tool.R' --input '${input_file}' --lon-col '${columns.lon_col}' --lat-col '${columns.lat_col}' --cluster-mode '${cluster_section.cluster_mode}' #if $cluster_section.cluster_mode == "auto" --buffer-km '${cluster_section.buffer_km}' #else --cluster-col '${cluster_section.cluster_col}' #end if #if $view_section.view_mode.view_mode_sel == "manual" --view-xmin '${view_section.view_mode.view_xmin}' --view-xmax '${view_section.view_mode.view_xmax}' --view-ymin '${view_section.view_mode.view_ymin}' --view-ymax '${view_section.view_mode.view_ymax}' #else if $view_section.view_mode.view_mode_sel == "countries" --view-countries '${view_section.view_mode.view_countries}' #end if --map-title '${map_options.map_title}'

param.name: input_file
param.label: Upload your data
param.help: Data in tabular format containing at least one longitude one latitude column.

param.name: lon_col
param.label: The exact name of the column containing the longitudes

param.name: lat_col
param.label: The exact name of the column containing the latitudes

param.name: cluster_mode
param.label: Method of defining populations
param.help: Choose whether the populations are defined automatically using a geographical buffer or manually via a column in the file.
param.option: Manual: population column provided in the file

param.name: buffer_km
param.label: Buffer radius in kilometres
param.help: Two individuals whose buffers touch are considered to belong to the same population. Therefore, two individuals belong to the same population if they are separated by twice the buffer radius.

param.name: cluster_col
param.label: The exact name of the column containing the populations/clusters

param.name: view_mode_sel
param.label: Window definition mode
param.help: By default, the window is calculated automatically around your points.

param.name: view_countries
param.label: Countries (separated by commas)
param.help: Names in English or ISO-2/ISO-3 codes. Examples: France, Spain, Portugal or FR, ES, PT

tool.help:
Usage =====
This tool generates a static geographic map of sampling points with automatic or manual population clustering. It takes a tabular file containing longitude and latitude coordinates on WGS84 referential and produces a map with colour-coded populations and ellipses representing each grou'ps spatial spread.
Two modes are available : - **Automatic clustering:** Individus are grouped using a circular buffer approach. Two individuals are assigned to the same population if the distance between them is less than or equal to twice the specified buffer radius (in km). This is implemented via DBSCAN. This mode also exports a summary table with the inferred population assignments appended to the original data. - **Manual clustering:** The user provides a column in the input file that already contains population labels. No distance computation is performed.
The map windows can be defined in three ways : automatically fitted around the data points with a configurable padding margin, restricted to a list of countries (by English name or ISO-2/ISO-3 code), or set manually using explicit longitude/latitude bounds. For countries with overseas territories (e.g. France, Spain), only the continental polygon is used to compute the bounding box; points falling outside are still included by expanding the window accordingly.
The tool generates a PNG map of the sampling points with poopulation ellipses and a tabular file with population assignments on automatic mode only.
